title: Meeting notes at speed
description: Real meeting notes — bullets, attendees, decisions
text: Meeting: Q3 Planning. Attendees: David, Sarah, Mark, Lisa. Decisions: 1) launch on August 15; 2) increase marketing budget by 20%; 3) hire two more engineers. Action items: Sarah owns the launch checklist; Mark drafts the budget proposal by Friday.

tips.text: Notes are written at the speed of the room. There is no second take while someone keeps talking.
Clipped fragments, colons, a stray number - that is what real notes look like. Catch the sense, not every word.

tips.target: Meeting: Q3 Planning. Attendees: David, Sarah, Mark, Lisa. Decisions: 1) launch on August 15; 2) increase marketing budget by

tips.text: Train this until your hands run on their own and your ears are free to listen. That is the whole skill of note-taking.

tips.target: 20%; 3) hire two more engineers. Action items: Sarah owns the launch checklist; Mark drafts the budget proposal by Friday.

character_tips.anna: Live notes are the hardest test of all - you write while the talking carries on. Master it and you are useful in any room. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Type faster than people speak and nothing slips past you. That is worth more than any certificate. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Don't chase every syllable. Get the decision and who owns it; the rest is noise. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Writing while you listen, calm and quick. Keep your eyes up and let your fingers keep pace. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
